sections.paragraph:
Σάρδεις, εων, αἱ (this spelling of the name Aeschyl., Thu. et al.; ins; SibOr 5, 289) Sardis, the ancient capital city of Lydia, in western Asia Minor Rv 1:11; 3:1, 4.—Ramsay, Letters 1905. Its inscriptions are found in Sardis: Publications of the American Soc. for the Excav. of Sardis VII ’32; on Jews in Sardis s. ISardRobert I, p. 38. Apollonius of Tyana wrote letters τοῖς ἐν Σάρδεσιν (nos. 38; 75f) and τοῖς Σαρδιανοῖς (56): Philostrat. I p. 353; 359; 366; SJohnson, Christianity in Sardis, HRWilloughby Festschr. ’61, 81–90.—Pauly-W. II 2475–78; Kl. Pauly IV 1551f; BHHW III 1670f; OEANE IV 484–88; PECS 808–810. CHemer in New Docs 3, 55f.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ramsay, Letters Ramsay, Letters = The Letters to the Seven Churches of Asia

ISardRobert ISardRobert = Nouvelles inscriptions de Sardes I, ed. LRobert—List 3

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

OEANE OEANE = Oxford Encyclopedia of Archaeology in the Near East—List 6

PECS PECS = Princeton Encyclopedia of Classical Sites—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 913
